title: System test for #9096 (truncated stdout)
body:
This actually tests conmon, not podman; but that's the whole
point of system tests in the first place: if a problem exists,
we want to fail loudly, no matter whose fault it is.

(I can't get this to fail on my f33 laptop; OP on #9096 claims
it only fails on Ubuntu. We'll see what happens in CI).

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@giuseppe @haircommander could you take a quick look and LMK if my test logic here is meaningful? Does the podman log output path exercise a close-enough code path to that of plain stdout?

I've pulled this in to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8979 where I'm seeing failures from conmon 2.0.25. Hopefully this fails there, and updating to conmon 2.0.26 will fix it, verifying this issue is fixed

@haircommander thank you! Although this PR has just merged, could you please follow up with your findings? (Right now I see #8979 stuck in all-red in `int` tests, which means the `sys` tests didn't get a chance to run)
